step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a serverless web application hosting a modeling platform at 3M. Users (scientists) connect via CloudFront, which uses Lambda@Edge to authenticate against a third-party IDP. The SPA is fetched from S3. The API layer is fronted by API Gateway, which targets ECS Fargate, backed by DynamoDB for configurations. For CI/CD model publishing, a Git repository triggers Step Functions with Lambdas, which copy model files to an HPC cluster running on EC2. Users submit jobs through the API layer (ECS), which triggers execution on the EC2 HPC cluster, which writes the output results back to S3.

graph.name: 3M serverless model publishing and HPC platform
graph.notes: Serverless architecture that automates ML/physics model publishing to an HPC cluster via step functions and Lambdas, with a secure frontend powered by ECS Fargate, Lambda@Edge, and DynamoDB.

nodes.name: Scientist / User
nodes.notes: 3M corporate scientists who build, publish, and run physics and ML models.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Distributes SPA and acts as the entry point for the web application.

nodes.name: Auth Edge Lambda
nodes.notes: Executes authentication checks and integrates with the external identity provider.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Hosts the SPA assets and stores completed HPC job results.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Secures and routes frontend API requests to the compute layer.

nodes.name: ECS Fargate
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Serverless API layer managing model configurations and initiating compute jobs.

nodes.name: DynamoDB Table
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores user access permissions and frontend configuration settings.

nodes.name: Git Repository
nodes.notes: Source control system holding model definitions and code, triggering Step Functions on update.

nodes.name: Model Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Coordinates serverless publishing workflows for models.

nodes.name: Publishing Lambdas
nodes.notes: Executes deployment steps choreographed by Step Functions.

nodes.name: EC2 HPC Cluster
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: High-performance computing cluster processing submitted physics and ML model jobs.

edges.notes: User initiates API call to run a model.
Routes API call to the serverless container backend.
Launches computing job on HPC cluster.
Saves job outputs and simulation results.
Scientist pushes model updates to Git.
Deploys model files into the HPC cluster directory.
